modules.module_title: NATP Module 6: Medical and Surgical Asepsis
modules.source_status_flag: Generated from approved Module 6 PDF source map.
learning_objectives: Name four infectious agents (microbes) and discuss the diseases they cause
State five conditions necessary for infectious agents to grow
Identify the six parts of the chain of infection
List four lines of defense against infection in the body
Describe the signs and symptoms of infection
Differentiate between medical and surgical asepsis
Identify the roles of the Centers for Disease Control (CDC) and the Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) in the prevention of infections
Describe personal protective equipment (PPE) and proper use
Identify the psychological effects of standard precaution and transmission based precautions on patient/patients/residents
